comment.body: so...in line with everything you've been saying its really best to continue to take a wait and see approach through the lens you've mentioned. Consider rangebound action, look for catalysts that can change the trajectory or testing of the bounds of the range we're in. Consider opex to be a generally supportive one but less so post-apex as those calls unwind. However, news can be a driver of positive outcomes that would change the potential for the kind of a pullback/correction we see. Its either a healthy pullback to continue ultimately in a bull market direction, or a transition to a bear market where things are heavily impacted by the dangers we know about lurking in the shadows with staglation/recessionary fears/China playing it's cards and making power moves to inflict max pain on trade talks, anything out of order with a truce in ukraine/russia conflict and elsewhere.

comment.body: Yeah, but it ain’t Alma’s fault. People here keep acting like rookie retail traders. These have been wild times, you can’t even imagine the chaos the funds have been through, and it isn’t over until we see real progress, like Alma keeps stressing. And don’t forget, she was the first I saw calling the “hidden QE” before opex, and she nailed it, way before Nomura or Goldman’s inside reports, while still warning about the very real risks. You’re just salty ’cause your FOMO’s kicking in. Plus, today she pointed out that Trump’s announcement was timed to halt a pullback, and that’s exactly what happened. Folks really need to learn how to interpret texts. You’ve all been handed tons of top-tier analysis
